title: FieldCase
description: A candidate field instance submitted for classification and consciousness stress-testing. Not a proof of consciousness. A falsification fixture.

id.description: Unique identifier for this fixture. Snake_case.

description.description: What this case represents and why it is submitted for testing.

field_type.description: Primary field type classification. Not a consciousness claim — field organization does not automatically imply phenomenal fielding.

phenomenal_candidate.description: Whether this case is being evaluated as a candidate for phenomenal consciousness. false = known non-candidate (false positive control). true = genuinely contested or under stress-test.

components.description: C = G · U · D · T · F. Each component scored 0.0–1.0 or null if genuinely unresolvable. If any non-null component is 0.0, C collapses. NOTE: G was previously labeled A in v0.x fixtures — same component, renamed for precision. G = givenness / appearance-from-within, treating it explicitly as the primitive residue where analysis stops.

G.description: Givenness / appearance-from-within. Is there something it is like to be this, from inside? 0 = no / clearly absent. 1 = strongly established. null = unresolvable by current tools. G is the primitive residue: the harness cannot detect G directly — it can only classify cases and flag G as established, absent, or unresolvable. Previously labeled A in v0.x fixtures.

A.description: DEPRECATED: Legacy alias for G (givenness). Used in v0.x fixtures. New fixtures should use G. Kept for backward compatibility.

U.description: Local unity / co-presence. Are internal states co-present in a unified field rather than merely parallel?

D.description: Differentiation / internal contrast. Does the field contain distinguishable qualitative states?

T.description: Temporal thickness. Does the field have a specious present — a now with retention and protention?

F.description: Field inclusion. Are states genuinely included in an active phenomenal field vs. merely processed?

c_score.description: Computed C score (A·U·D·T·F). null if any component is null. Preserved for audit. Not a consciousness proof.

verdict.description: Classification verdict after stress-testing. demoted = was a candidate but failed a test. unresolved = test could not settle the question.

revision_pressure.description: What this case puts pressure on in the theory. Where does the theory need refinement based on this fixture?

breaks_preserved.description: Any breaks this case produced in the theory that must NOT be patched with prose. Each entry is a specific unresolved tension.

source_anchors.description: Literature / empirical references for this case.

notes.description: Free text notes. Used for unresolved nuance, competing interpretations, or future test directions.
